metadata.content_note: First Grade 5 English production proof SkillPackage only.

subject: English
skill: Multisyllable Word Reading, Roots, and Affixes

lesson.story_mission: Enter the Word Observatory, where long academic words become readable when you split syllables, identify roots and affixes, and blend the parts with meaning.
lesson.objective: I can decode and understand multisyllable words using syllable patterns, prefixes, suffixes, and Greek and Latin roots.
lesson.concept_explanation: Fifth-grade readers do not need to guess at long words. They scan for prefixes and suffixes, find Greek or Latin roots, divide the remaining word into readable syllables, pronounce each part, blend the word smoothly, and check the meaning in context.
lesson.common_misconception: A reader may divide syllables in the wrong place, mix up affix meanings, forget a root meaning, or guess a multisyllable word from its beginning instead of decoding every meaningful part.
lesson.real_world_connection: Word analysis helps you read science words like photosynthesis, social studies words like transportation, and new book vocabulary independently.
lesson.lesson_summary: Look for affixes and roots, split the rest into syllables, read every part, blend the word, and check the meaning.
lesson.mini_lesson: Step 1: circle any prefix or suffix. Step 2: underline the root or base word. Step 3: divide the rest into syllables. Step 4: pronounce each part, blend the whole word, and ask whether the meaning fits.

audio.text: multisyllable, prefix, suffix, roots, affixes, morphology, pronunciation
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the word or word parts with me.

worked_examples.text: Watch: observation splits into ob | ser | va | tion. Read each syllable, then blend: observation.

audio.text: observation. ob, ser, va, tion. observation.
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the word or word parts with me.

worked_examples.text: Demo: preview has pre- plus view. Pre- means before, so preview means to view before.

audio.text: preview. pre means before. view means look at. preview means view before.
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the word or word parts with me.

worked_examples.text: Practice: biography uses bio, meaning life, and graph, meaning write. A biography is writing about a life.

audio.text: biography. bio means life. graph means write. biography.
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the word or word parts with me.

worked_examples.text: Challenge: irresponsible is built from ir-, response, and -ible. Ir- means not, and -ible means able to be.

audio.text: ir plus response plus ible makes irresponsible.
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the word or word parts with me.

hint_ladder: Slow down and look for meaningful word parts before you guess.
Mark syllables, prefixes, suffixes, roots, or base words. Then read one part at a time.
Blend the parts and check that the meaning fits the sentence or word-building clue.

syllable_division_error.description: The reader splits a multisyllable word at an unhelpful place or ignores vowel patterns.
syllable_division_error.teacher_move: Have the learner clap syllables, mark vowels, and read each chunk before blending.

affix_meaning_confusion.description: The learner confuses a prefix or suffix meaning, such as pre- with re- or -able with -ful.
affix_meaning_confusion.teacher_move: Use prefix/base/suffix tiles and ask what each affix contributes to the word.

root_meaning_confusion.description: The learner memorizes the whole word but misses the Greek or Latin root meaning.
root_meaning_confusion.teacher_move: Compare several words with the same root and name the shared meaning.

multisyllable_guessing.description: The learner guesses from the first letters instead of decoding all word parts.
multisyllable_guessing.teacher_move: Cover and reveal the word part by part; require read, blend, and meaning-check steps.

audio.text: port means carry, as in transport and portable.
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the word or word parts with me.

audio.text: mis means wrong or badly, as in misread and misunderstand.

game_theme.title: Word Observatory
game_theme.name: Word Observatory
game_theme.mission_intro: Decode the Observatory's long word signals by using syllables, roots, affixes, and morphology.

story.text: Welcome to the Word Observatory. Today you will learn how expert readers handle long words without guessing. You will use syllables, roots, prefixes, and suffixes like tools on a star map.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches a repeatable decoding routine. First find prefixes and suffixes. Then look for a Greek or Latin root. Next split the remaining word into syllables, read every part, blend the word, and check the meaning.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch carefully as the tutor breaks a long word into readable chunks. Notice that the goal is not just to pronounce the word; it is also to understand what the parts mean.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: Now try the routine with guidance. Say each word part, name what it means when you can, and blend the full word smoothly before choosing an answer.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: In practice, pause before answering. Use the visual model to split, build, or explain the word. Reading all parts prevents multisyllable guessing.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: The challenge asks you to combine decoding and meaning. Use roots and affixes to make a smart choice, then check that the pronunciation and meaning both work.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This checkpoint checks whether you can apply the word analysis routine independently. Read the question, study the word parts, answer, and explain your thinking to yourself.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: You earned the Word Analysis Navigator badge when you showed that long words can be decoded part by part. Keep using this routine in science, social studies, and novels.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: Your profile will remember this skill as word analysis practice. Continue to the Skill Practice Center to build speed, accuracy, and confidence with new multisyllable words.
profile.label: Read This Page

level_banks.label: Level 1: Syllable Patterns
level_banks.focus: Use syllable patterns to divide and pronounce Grade 5 multisyllable words.

questions.prompt: Which syllable break helps you read evaporation?
hints: Slow down and look for meaningful word parts before you guess.
Mark syllables, prefixes, suffixes, roots, or base words. Then read one part at a time.
Blend the parts and check that the meaning fits the sentence or word-building clue.
questions.explanation: The answer is e-vap-o-ra-tion. Use the word parts first, then blend and check meaning.
questions.feedback: Strong word analysis. You used the parts of the word instead of guessing.

question_audio.text: Which syllable break helps you read evaporation? Choices: e-vap-o-ra-tion, ev-aporation, evaporati-on.
question_audio.label: Read Question

audio.text: evaporation. e, vap, o, ra, tion. evaporation.
audio.label: Listen
